India U19 World Cup 2026: USA Clash Highlights

India U19 World Cup 2026: USA Clash Highlights

India U19 will begin their 2026 World Cup campaign against the USA, setting the stage for an exciting tournament in Zimbabwe and Namibia. The ICC has announced the fixtures for the U19 Men’s Cricket World Cup, scheduled from January 15 to February 6, with the final to be played in Harare.

Opening Day Fixtures and Key Matches

The tournament opens with a packed schedule, including:

  • Last edition’s runners-up India U19 vs USA
  • Zimbabwe vs Scotland
  • Tanzania making their global debut against West Indies
  • Reigning champions Australia vs Ireland in Windhoek on January 16

Matches will be held across five venues: Harare Sports Club, Takashinga, and Queens Sports Club in Zimbabwe, as well as Namibia Cricket Ground and HP Oval in Windhoek. Warm-up matches are scheduled from January 9 to 14.

Tournament Structure and Groups

The U19 World Cup 2026 follows four groups, a Super Six stage, two semi-finals, and the final. The groups are:

Group A: India, Bangladesh, USA, New Zealand
Group B: Zimbabwe, Pakistan, England, Scotland
Group C: Australia, Ireland, Japan, Sri Lanka
Group D: Tanzania, West Indies, Afghanistan, South Africa

ICC Chairman on U19 World Cup Impact

ICC Chairman Jay Shah emphasized:
The ICC U19 Men’s Cricket World Cup has long been the cradle of greatness, revealing not just the next generation of cricketers, but the next generation of icons. From Brian Lara to Virat Kohli and Shubman Gill, this tournament consistently shapes the future of our sport.

Tanzania’s Historic Debut

Tanzania will make their first appearance in a global ICC event, debuting against the West Indies. This highlights the tournament’s inclusivity and provides young players with invaluable international exposure.

Preparing for a World-Class Tournament

The 2026 U19 World Cup promises a world-class platform that mirrors senior international cricket standards. Young cricketers will experience high-level competition, intense rivalries, and opportunities to showcase their talent globally.

With the final scheduled for February 6 in Harare, teams are preparing rigorously. Fans can expect thrilling matches, breakout stars, and the continuation of the U19 legacy in producing the next generation of cricketing icons.
